settings.title: Raffia
settings.text:
Woven raffia is a light natural material for the city and warmer seasons. Keep it away from heavy moisture.

If wet, gently blot with a soft dry cloth and let dry naturally away from heaters and direct sun.

settings.title: Intreccio leather
settings.text:
Natural leather and intreccio weaving keep their grain, softness and shade. Small variations are a sign of a living material.

Do not machine-wash or soak bags. Clean with a soft cloth and leather care products.

settings.title: Silk / Foulard
settings.text:
Silk scarves add a light accent to a bag or look. Store them away from direct sun to keep the print vivid.

Prefer gentle dry cleaning or hand wash in cool water with silk detergent.

settings.title: Care and storage
settings.text:
Before long-term storage, clean and fully dry the piece. Fill the bag with soft paper and keep it in a fabric dust bag.

Avoid plastic bags for long storage. Store detachable straps inside the bag.

settings.question: Can I get a raffia or leather bag wet?
settings.answer: No — leather and raffia pieces should not be exposed to moisture. If wet, blot with a dry cloth and let dry naturally.

settings.question: How do I clean a leather bag?
settings.answer: Use a soft cloth and leather care products. Test on a hidden area first, then wipe dry.

settings.question: How should I store my bag?
settings.answer: In a fabric dust bag, filled with soft paper to keep the shape, away from direct sun, high humidity and plastic bags.
